ending: A mountain strike destroys most of the defended city, but Olive, Mayalyn, Markhiss, and the allied peoples establish a surviving line. The emperors and rankers separate the Graymin king from its horde, stopping its production of new beasts. A Church artifact detonates against the king, leaving it maimed but alive and moving north. Jiran prevents a second mountain launch, only to be trapped by Graymin and captured by the temple agent Three. A bone restraint remains embedded in him, yet his Remalon body lets him resist its mana drain. Jiran and Three accept a mutual non-harm agreement in exchange for ten unspecified tasks. Jiran then absorbs his stored Challenger density and loses consciousness during the transformation. An unseen scarlet rift pulls both men into an unknown red-energy realm. Jiran's destination, the collar, the contract, and the Challenger trial remain unresolved. Niya, Mayalyn, Olive, Cameron, Lulu, Markhiss, and the coalition remain behind amid a shattered defense. Elsewhere, a Remalon leader begins searching for a Tier 4 anomaly whose description strongly points to Jiran, but she expects the trial to kill the fugitive.
in_short: Jiran rescues the Timberlings from exploitation, uncovers their shared spiritual origin, and equips them to defend themselves. Preparing for an unprecedented Graymin assault, he allies with former enemies, secures long-term service from most of their warriors, and brings multiple peoples through his portals to reinforce Olive's defense of Melathon. He supplies mana, weapons, formations, healing, and elemental instruction while the Graymin king drives and replenishes an enormous horde. Church and shadow factions obstruct Imperial reinforcements and seek control of Jiran's gateways. Markhiss defects to Jiran and exposes their plan. A mountain devastates the city, though its defenders survive and the king is maimed by a Church artifact. After stopping a second mountain strike, Jiran is captured by the temple agent Three. They make a mutual non-harm agreement that leaves Jiran owing ten tasks. Jiran absorbs his Challenger density, falls unconscious, and vanishes with Three through a scarlet rift. The Remalon leadership has also begun searching for an apparent Tier 4 anomaly matching him.

recaps.text:
Previously: Jiran became a champion connected to the sentient world Madra and evolved into a Tier 4 Remalon. His unusual mana control, elemental knowledge, healing, and access to an interface set him apart from the Empire's established powers. Niya, his cousin, and Mayalyn, his partner, remained his closest traveling companions, while Olive and Cameron were part of his wider circle of allies. Daughter existed within Jiran's soul as an uncertain link to Madra. Jiran also carried obligations to mentors including Lenton and Feylon Samris, whose inherited curses had not been cured.

Jiran's identity was politically sensitive. Imperial and Church interests viewed his knowledge as strategically important. The Graymin remained the Empire's ancient enemy, and earlier dangers had left Jiran alert to both open war and hidden factions.

Jiran, Niya, and Mayalyn enter a Timberling community that regards Jiran as the Great Spirit of a troubling prophecy. He refuses to exploit that belief. With his Challenger timer still active, he is also seeking powerful opponents. When he discovers a girl mutilated and abandoned by her sect, he restores her limbs and gives her the new name Lulu. She becomes the party's guide. The Timberlings live under rigid population controls and fear winged hive hunters that carry their people away.

The hunters prove to be a sapient winged people. A dissenter explains that the dominant Stormclaw clan farms the Timberlings and kills them to support safe advancement. Jiran captures nearly two hundred valley residents, leaving the Timberlings to judge them while he prepares for retaliation. He also begins teaching Niya healing and develops a primitive teleportation method. Daughter warns that divination has failed across much of the Empire. In the capital, Olive, Cameron, and Emperor Dominus learn that the Graymin have unexpectedly retreated from every front. Seeking greater strength, Jiran enters the Land of the Lost to hunt Tier 7 fog predators, only to find several waiting in a forest that distorts perception and repairs itself through its fog.

Jiran kills Tier 7 fog predators and returns with proof of his strength. He defeats a Timberling challenge but refuses to let either side execute prisoners without understanding the consequences. The twelve matrons ultimately choose death for the captured winged people. Their unified Harmony kills the prisoners and nearly kills every connected Timberling, but Jiran shields Lulu, revives the matrons, and saves the other victims.

The seven ancient elders explain that Harmony travels through the Timberlings' shared song. Their first mother then speaks through them, saying that divine beings shattered her soul after tearing her from another world. The elders are preserved fragments of that soul, and the present Timberlings appear to carry the remaining pieces. She entrusts her descendants to Jiran and warns of a coming king. The elders collapse into seven living heartwoods, which Lulu must protect and eventually cultivate.

Meanwhile, Jiran discovers Sanctuary, a crystal structure in space operating at only eight percent power. Its functioning stasis chamber holds an unidentified blonde child designated DH0001, but the group lacks the energy to revive her. Lulu forms a new sect devoted to Jiran and becomes its matron. Jiran accepts responsibility for protecting the Timberlings and orders their full mobilization.

Melathon faces a Graymin army of roughly twenty million and a mountain-sized king that once defeated the emperors. Olive refuses to abandon the northern city and prepares to command its defense. In the valley, Jiran asks the Timberlings to supply mana for Sanctuary and warns that new corrupted beasts threaten their scouts. Knife loses an arm while saving her group from an ambush, but Mayalyn rescues her, Niya stabilizes her, and Jiran restores the limb.

Jiran builds graphene-reinforced armor and formations that let him hunt Tier 7 fog beasts efficiently, purify their mana, and store the surplus. The Challenger density he gains also produces violent impulses that he must consciously manage. He encounters leaders of the winged Rising Sky faction, distinguishes them from the murderous Divine faction, and teaches four elders how to hunt in the Land of the Lost. They begin forming an alliance against Stormclaw.

By safely siphoning voluntary Timberling mana, Niya earns an introductory interface and joins Jiran's party. She gains limited control of Sanctuary functions and can independently open a portal to the Empire. Jiran and Niya recognize that this transport access and the crystals enabling it must remain restricted to trusted allies.

Jiran uses Mana Omnis and Mana Confluence to create weapons that let Timberlings channel their internal mana, then reinforces their bark with graphene. Mayalyn insists that her own people must be allowed to choose whether to fight for the Empire, and she and Jiran openly affirm their love. Olive defeats a Tier 6 general in a duel and takes operational command at Melathon, where the defense is intended to delay rather than defeat the approaching horde.

The conflict among the winged clans reaches its conclusion. Rising Sky elders trap the Stormclaw and Divine leaders in the Land of the Lost, and Jiran kills the Stormclaw leader. He rescues the dead leader's twin children, heals them, and spares them after they agree to reform their clan and protect the Timberlings. The masked Eldest Unspeakable then brokers a wider settlement. Jiran gains command of sixty percent of the winged warriors for one thousand years in exchange for equipment and protection. Even past killers can earn conditional pardon through service and beast hunting.

The Eldest Unspeakable is a Challenger damaged by centuries of depleted willpower, which prevents him from joining the war. At Melathon's War Council, Lenton reveals that Mortimer the Fireling is Jiran. The Empire's highest war leaders now know his hidden identity and value his knowledge for the coming defense.

Jiran equips and rallies the Timberlings, Mayalyn's people, and his winged allies for Melathon. He accepts a protector role among Mayalyn's people and brings Knife as a field companion while keeping Lulu away from direct combat. Olive completes a defensive bottleneck around the city with help from Lenton and a Tier 10 formation specialist. When the Graymin advance speaks with one voice and demands an unidentified man, the defenders realize the horde is acting intelligently.

Jiran arrives before the line collapses and breaks the first assault, but all five emperors pull him into a private meeting. They fear both the Graymin king and a later purge, and want his portals to evacuate the population and establish a successor realm. Jiran refuses to abandon the battle. Instead, he opens routes for allied reinforcements, draws mana from seasonal clouds, and learns to alter donated high-tier mana so recipients can safely use it. More than ten thousand winged warriors halt the immediate advance while the city's formations come online.

Jiran then transports the Timberlings and Mayalyn's people to the rear flanks. He shares elemental principles that greatly increase the rankers' destructive power, heals allied casualties, and coordinates with Olive to divide their coalition across both fronts. The defense is organized, but the main enemy force is still approaching.

Jiran and Olive conclude that the nearby Graymin king explains the horde's speech and coordination. Jiran's improved weapons, mana transfers, gravity attacks, and portal reinforcements turn the coalition into a sustainable defense. Timberling weapons reshape the battlefield with ice walls, while new equipment lets the winged forces recover mana from their own attacks. Olive commands the Imperial west and the winged allies hold the east. The emperors and rankers depart to contain the king, leaving Olive and Jiran responsible for the lower tiers.

Hidden enemies move at the same time. An Imperial princess warns Jiran about a shadow organization seeking to exploit his gateways. Church leaders plan to seize a portal and prepare a catastrophic artifact that could destroy the Empire. Their assassins, alongside the shadow faction's agents, have prevented reinforcements from reaching the city. Markhiss hunts those operatives while struggling with intrusive voices. He eventually submits his aura to Jiran, who removes one skill under Mayalyn's protection. The voices stop for the moment, and Markhiss swears not to betray Jiran, though the cure is not yet certain. He reveals the two factions' plan to let the Graymin cripple the Imperial army before escaping. Elsewhere, an Imperial prince reaches an emptied refugee cave and is confronted by a superior hostile force.

Graymin rooks throw a mountain into the defended city, destroying its walls and most buildings. Jiran, Mayalyn, Olive, Markhiss, and the coalition rescue survivors and establish a makeshift line. Jiran goes alone to stop about half a million rooks from launching a second mountain. He collapses their excavation, but a specialized force of Tier 7 Graymin traps him. Temple agents enter the battle, and the Tier 9 agent Three captures Jiran alive with a regenerative bone collar that blocks his external mana control.

Jiran's Remalon body and an unknown internal force resist the restraint. After surviving severe burns, he negotiates a mutual agreement: Three will not harm Jiran or his allies, and Jiran will complete ten unspecified tasks. Stored Challenger density then floods into Jiran and remakes his body. While he is unconscious, an undetectable scarlet rift pulls him and Three into an unknown red-energy realm.

At the king's battlefield, the emperors learn that separating the Graymin king from its horde stops it from producing more beasts. A Church artifact detonates against the king, maiming but not killing it, and drives it north. The city survives in ruins, while Jiran's allies remain behind. A Remalon leader has begun hunting a Tier 4 anomaly strongly indicated to be Jiran, but assumes his Challenger trial will be fatal.
